creative_brief: Hero image: overhead shot of a real farmer's weathered hands holding produce, with a FarmThru collection hub softly visible in the background. Earthy, honest palette — no glossy supermarket lighting. Typography feels handwritten-meets-editorial. Mood: confession, not pitch.
headline: I sold groceries I couldn't fully trace. That stops now.
subhead: Most grocery stores — even the good ones — can't name the farm behind every product. We built FarmThru so you'd never have to wonder. Now you can own a piece of a grocery store that pays farmers first.
hero_copy: Here's what I didn't want to admit: when we started, even we couldn't guarantee every supply chain was as short as we claimed. So we stripped it back. Zero warehouses. Zero wholesalers. Farm to hub to you. Today, every product on FarmThru traces to a named farm — Bundarra for pork, Paris Creek for carbon-neutral dairy, Collins Wild Caught for South Coast seafood. Around 15–20 partner farms across NSW and beyond. That transparency cost us margin. It earned us something better: trust.

sections.heading: The system wasn't built for honesty
sections.body: Supermarket supply chains average 2,400km before food hits the shelf. That distance isn't just fuel — it's where accountability disappears. A label says 'Australian grown' but won't name the farmer, the practice, or whether the land was left better after harvest. Regenerative farming rebuilds soil, sequesters carbon, and treats animals like living things — not units. But the current system punishes farmers who farm this way. They get the same wholesale rate as industrial operations. FarmThru's hub-and-collect model cuts the chain short: farm to our Brookvale hub to your hands. Farmers set fair prices. You see exactly who grew what. No middlemen skimming margin that should stay on the land.

sections.heading: Named farms, not marketing claims
sections.body: Rachel Ward — yes, the actor — left that world behind to build Rachel's Farm in Kempsey, NSW. Regenerative cattle, real soil recovery. She's a key partner and supplier to FarmThru. Farmer Brown supplies our eggs. Little Yarran Farm is in our network. Collins Wild Caught brings wild-caught Alaskan salmon and seasonal Australian seafood from the South Coast. These aren't brands we white-label. They're farming families whose names sit next to every product. You order online, collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday, or get delivery across Sydney, Central Coast, and Wollongong. No subscription. No lock-in. No membership fees. Order when you want. Buy in bulk and save more.

sections.heading: Own a piece of the grocery store that pays farmers first
sections.body: We're opening equity crowdfunding so everyday people can become co-owners of FarmThru. Not customers only — partners. Your values and your money finally pointing the same direction. This isn't charity. It's a bet that a fairer food system can also be a viable business. Join the waitlist to be first notified when the campaign goes live. You'll also be first to shop when new hubs open. Investing in early-stage businesses carries real risk, including the possibility of losing your entire investment. But if you believe supermarkets shouldn't be the only option — and that farmers deserve more than wholesale scraps — this is your chance to be part of building the alternative.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru equity crowdfunding is conducted under Australian crowd-sourced funding (CSF) regulations. Investing in early-stage businesses is speculative and carries significant risk, including the risk of losing your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. No returns are guaranteed. This page is not financial advice. Please read the offer document carefully before making any investment decision.
